message:
feat(pane-terminal): add force-cancel safety valve and RAII interaction guard (bd-24v9m)

Add force_cancel() to PaneDragResizeMachine for unconditional reset to Idle,
force_cancel_all() and PaneCleanupDiagnostics to PaneTerminalAdapter for
structured cleanup, and PaneInteractionGuard RAII wrapper that force-cancels
on drop. 17 new tests across ftui-layout and ftui-runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-24g4","title":"fix: markup hyperlink state restoration bug with interleaved tags","description":"Fixed bug in pop_style() where link URLs were incorrectly restored when closing non-link tags that were opened inside a link. Example: [link=a][bold]text[/link]more[/bold]after - 'after' incorrectly got the link. Root cause: entry.link_url captured at push time became stale when link closed. Fix: derive link state from remaining stack entries instead of restoring from popped entry.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:18:34.512860386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T18:18:40.069318957Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T18:18:40.069298097Z","close_reason":"Fixed: Changed pop_style() to derive link state from remaining stack entries instead of restoring potentially stale entry.link_url. Added test parse_link_with_nested_tag_outlasting_link. All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-255jd","title":"Parser: HTS/TBC/CBT tab stop sequences + DECKPAM/DECKPNM keypad modes + conformance fixtures","description":"## Gap Analysis

frankenterm-core cursor.rs has full tab stop logic (set_tab_stop, clear_tab_stop, clear_all_tab_stops, next_tab_stop, prev_tab_stop) but the parser has NO action variants for:
- HTS (ESC H) - Set Tab Stop at current column
- TBC (CSI Ps g) - Tab Clear (0=at cursor, 3=all)
- CBT (CSI Ps Z) - Cursor Backward Tabulation
- DECKPAM (ESC =) - Application Keypad Mode
- DECKPNM (ESC >) - Normal Keypad Mode

## Deliverables
1. Add Action variants: SetTabStop, ClearTabStop(u16), BackTab(u16), ApplicationKeypad, NormalKeypad
2. Wire parser to emit these from ESC and CSI decoders
3. Add conformance fixtures for tab stop manipulation and backward tab
4. Extend differential_terminal.rs to apply these actions
5. Add tests for all new sequences

## Acceptance Criteria
- Parser emits correct actions for ESC H, CSI g, CSI Z, ESC =, ESC >
- Conformance fixtures pass for tab operations (set/clear/backward)
- cargo check, clippy, fmt all pass
- Existing 71 parser tests + new tests all green","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T02:05:24.375944945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:17:44.720488810Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T02:15:03.772600440Z","close_reason":"Completed: parser HTS/TBC/CBT + keypad actions are wired, conformance fixtures exist, and differential/proptest harnesses now apply the new actions; all gates green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-255jd","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T02:05:37.343149905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-25df9","title":"Mermaid Link/Click Rendering + Hyperlink Policy","description":"Background
- Mermaid supports click/link directives and URLs in nodes/edges.
- Terminal rendering must present links safely and deterministically with graceful fallbacks.

Scope
- Define link policy: allow/deny, sanitize URLs, and map to terminal hyperlink sequences if supported.
- Expose config options: enable_links, link_mode (inline/footnote/off), sanitize_mode (strict/lenient).
- Provide fallback rendering: inline link markers + footnote legend or tooltip panel (legend layout via bd-1oa1y).
- Support linkStyle directives where feasible (underline/accent) in terminal constraints.
- Log link_count, link_mode, and sanitization outcomes to JSONL.

Considerations
- Security: no external fetches, sanitize URLs, and prevent escape injection.
- Deterministic output across capability profiles; same input yields same link_mode.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for link parsing, sanitization, and policy decisions.
- Snapshot tests for hyperlink-capable and ASCII fallback modes (inline/footnote).
- PTY E2E logs include link_count and link_mode.

Success Criteria
- Links render safely and consistently; unsupported modes degrade predictably.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueOtter","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:14:06.991818179Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:39:06.780070987Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T06:39:06.779969245Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-25df9","depends_on_id":"bd-1ri6i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:14:13.525712086Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25df9","depends_on_id":"bd-315zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:14:13.983347276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25df9","depends_on_id":"bd-3monr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:14:13.759637186Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25df9","depends_on_id":"bd-j20xh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:14:14.211052076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25df9","depends_on_id":"bd-pt0dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:14:13.293606691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-25epb","title":"Failing target: ftui-harness isomorphism_proofs","description":"Scope: fix isomorphism_proofs test failures to allow coverage runs.","acceptance_criteria":"1) isomorphism_proofs test passes in coverage mode.\n2) Root cause documented with logs or minimal repro and evidence ledger.\n3) Fix includes deterministic tests with rich assertion context.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:15:43.770478963Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T20:18:58.680622379Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T20:18:58.680598795Z","close_reason":"Coverage-aware perf budget + PIPE-1 compare; isomorphism_proofs passes locally","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.4.5","title":"task(test): add selection resilience stress suite under churn","description":"Selection stability tests under heavy output, scrolling, and resize churn.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior in this task is deterministic for fixed inputs and capability profile.
- Required unit and/or E2E tests are added for the scoped behavior.
- Scope is complete only when dependencies remain satisfied and no regressions are introduced.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This task must add or update focused unit tests for core logic touched.
- This task must link to at least one end-to-end script path that validates integrated behavior.
- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Comprehensive unit tests are implemented for the scoped subsystem, including edge cases and regression vectors.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are implemented for the scoped workflows and runnable in CI.
- Test execution emits detailed structured logs (JSONL) including timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context.
- At least one deliberate failure injection scenario is covered and produces actionable diagnostics.","notes":"Execution Notes
- Selection logic should live in deterministic core state, not browser-only side effects.
- Ensure extraction rules are documented and fixture-backed for wraps, unicode, and trailing-space policy.
- Clipboard integration must have clear host callback boundaries and explicit fallback behavior.
- Stress cases must include simultaneous output churn and viewport movement.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:43.885171042Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:28.452112940Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","reliability","selection","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:43.885171042Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:16.906628928Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:17.285865473Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:17.664557178Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.5","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): terminal serialization and export APIs (ANSI/HTML/text)","description":"Background
- Export APIs are essential for logs, support artifacts, snapshots, and migration workflows.
- Mature terminals expose practical serialization options beyond on-screen rendering.

Goal
- Provide deterministic and documented serialization APIs for ANSI, HTML, and plain text.

In Scope
- API contract and options schema.
- ANSI serializer with style fidelity.
- HTML serializer with safe escaping and hyperlink fidelity.
- Plain-text serializer with clear wrap/space policy.
- Golden test matrix to prevent drift.

Out of Scope
- Ad hoc export behavior without explicit option contracts.

Why This Matters
- Serialization enables ecosystem workflows and strengthens debug/test ergonomics.

Acceptance Criteria
- Export outputs are deterministic for fixed input snapshots.
- Golden fixtures cover representative styling/unicode cases.
- API semantics are documented and versioned.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Feature behavior is validated by comprehensive unit tests that cover core invariants and edge cases.
- Feature behavior is validated by end-to-end test scripts that exercise realistic user workflows.
- E2E runs emit detailed structured logs (JSONL or equivalent) sufficient for deterministic failure triage and replay.
- CI gates for this feature fail on regression in correctness, determinism, or declared standards scope.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:35.145281825Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:15.418489448Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","export","frankenterm","serialization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:35.145281825Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:49:04.363130491Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.5.1","title":"task(serialization): define API contract and option schema for export methods","description":"Finalize method signatures and options for ANSI/HTML/text export paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior in this task is deterministic for fixed inputs and capability profile.
- Required unit and/or E2E tests are added for the scoped behavior.
- Scope is complete only when dependencies remain satisfied and no regressions are introduced.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This task must add or update focused unit tests for core logic touched.
- This task must link to at least one end-to-end script path that validates integrated behavior.
- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- ANSI/HTML/text export APIs are documented and deterministic.
- Golden fixtures cover representative style and unicode cases.
- Drift detection catches output regressions in CI.
- Export behavior matches declared options and defaults.

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","notes":"Execution Notes
- Serialization formats should be deterministic and suitable for diff-based regression testing.
- Prefer explicit option contracts over implicit format behavior.
- HTML export must prioritize safety and predictable structure.
- Golden tests should be shared across formats where possible to reduce fixture drift.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:44.270040896Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:48:21.171971288Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T04:48:21.171952443Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","frankenterm","serialization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:44.270040896Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-wo2","title":"Write Agent Harness Tutorial documentation","description":"# Write Agent Harness Tutorial Documentation

## Background & Strategic Importance
The plan explicitly requires documentation that includes an "Agent harness tutorial". This is THE PRIMARY documentation goal because agent harness UIs are ftui's core use case. Every design decision in ftui has been made with this tutorial's success in mind.

## Why This Documentation Is Critical
- FrankenTUI exists specifically for Claude Code / Codex-style agent harness UIs
- Without clear documentation, users cannot adopt ftui for their agents
- The tutorial IS the marketing - if someone can follow it, they understand ftui's value
- Plan Section 17 (Playbook) emphasizes this as a key deliverable

---

## Tutorial Structure

### Part 1: Hello World Harness (< 50 LOC)

**Goal:** Demonstrate the absolute minimum harness that compiles and runs.

```rust
//! Part 1: Hello World Harness
//!
//! This minimal example shows ftui's core pattern:
//! Model → update → view cycle with inline mode.

use ftui::prelude::*;

/// Our application state - just a counter for now.
struct HelloHarness {
    message: String,
}

impl Model for HelloHarness {
    type Message = ();

    fn init(&mut self) -> Cmd<Self::Message> {
        Cmd::none()
    }

    fn update(&mut self, _msg: Self::Message) -> Cmd<Self::Message> {
        Cmd::quit()
    }

    fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
        let area = frame.buffer.area();
        frame.buffer.set_string(0, 0, &self.message, Style::default());
    }
}

fn main() -> ftui::Result<()> {
    App::new(HelloHarness {
        message: "Hello from ftui!".to_string(),
    })
    .screen_mode(ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height: 1 })
    .run()
}
```

**Key concepts introduced:**
- `Model` trait with `init()`, `update()`, `view()`
- `Frame` and `Buffer` for rendering
- `ScreenMode::Inline` for scrollback preservation
- `Cmd` for commands (just `none()` and `quit()` here)

---

### Part 2: Adding Log Streaming

**Goal:** Build on Part 1 to show logs scrolling in scrollback.

```rust
//! Part 2: Log Streaming
//!
//! Demonstrates the `write_log()` pattern where log lines
//! enter native terminal scrollback while UI stays pinned.

use ftui::prelude::*;
use ftui_widgets::LogViewer;

struct LogHarness {
    log_viewer: LogViewer,
    log_count: usize,
}

#[derive(Clone)]
enum Msg {
    AddLog(String),
    Tick,
    Quit,
}

impl Model for LogHarness {
    type Message = Msg;

    fn init(&mut self) -> Cmd<Msg> {
        // Start a tick every 100ms to simulate incoming logs
        Cmd::tick(Duration::from_millis(100), || Msg::Tick)
    }

    fn update(&mut self, msg: Msg) -> Cmd<Msg> {
        match msg {
            Msg::AddLog(line) => {
                self.log_viewer.push(line);
            }
            Msg::Tick => {
                self.log_count += 1;
                self.log_viewer.push(format!("[{}] Log entry simulated", self.log_count));
                if self.log_count >= 100 {
                    return Cmd::quit();
                }
            }
            Msg::Quit => return Cmd::quit(),
        }
        Cmd::none()
    }

    fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
        // The log viewer fills the available area
        // In inline mode, this is the fixed UI region at the bottom
        let area = frame.buffer.area();
        let mut state = LogViewerState::default();
        self.log_viewer.render(area, &mut frame.buffer, &mut state);
    }
}
```

**Key concepts introduced:**
- `LogViewer` widget for streaming logs
- Tick-based updates for simulating incoming data
- Message enum pattern
- State management across renders

---

### Part 3: Interactive Input

**Goal:** Add user interaction with text input.

```rust
//! Part 3: Interactive Input
//!
//! Shows how to handle keyboard events and text input.

use ftui::prelude::*;
use ftui_widgets::{LogViewer, TextInput};

struct InteractiveHarness {
    log_viewer: LogViewer,
    input: TextInput,
    focused: bool,
}

#[derive(Clone)]
enum Msg {
    Key(KeyEvent),
    Submit(String),
    Tick,
}

impl Model for InteractiveHarness {
    type Message = Msg;

    fn init(&mut self) -> Cmd<Msg> {
        Cmd::batch(vec![
            Cmd::tick(Duration::from_millis(100), || Msg::Tick),
            Cmd::listen_keys(|k| Msg::Key(k)),
        ])
    }

    fn update(&mut self, msg: Msg) -> Cmd<Msg> {
        match msg {
            Msg::Key(key) => {
                match key.code {
                    KeyCode::Enter => {
                        let value = self.input.value().to_string();
                        self.input.clear();
                        return Cmd::msg(Msg::Submit(value));
                    }
                    KeyCode::Char('q') if key.modifiers.contains(Modifiers::CTRL) => {
                        return Cmd::quit();
                    }
                    KeyCode::Char(c) => self.input.insert_char(c),
                    KeyCode::Backspace => self.input.delete_char_before(),
                    KeyCode::Left => self.input.move_cursor_left(),
                    KeyCode::Right => self.input.move_cursor_right(),
                    _ => {}
                }
            }
            Msg::Submit(command) => {
                self.log_viewer.push(format!("> {}", command));
                self.log_viewer.push(format!("Executed: {}", command));
            }
            Msg::Tick => {
                // Spinner animation, status updates, etc.
            }
        }
        Cmd::none()
    }

    fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
        let area = frame.buffer.area();

        // Log viewer takes most of the space
        let log_area = Rect::new(0, 0, area.width, area.height - 1);
        let mut log_state = LogViewerState::default();
        self.log_viewer.render(log_area, &mut frame.buffer, &mut log_state);

        // Input line at bottom
        let input_area = Rect::new(0, area.height - 1, area.width, 1);
        frame.buffer.set_string(0, input_area.y, "> ", Style::default());
        let text_area = Rect::new(2, input_area.y, area.width - 2, 1);
        let mut input_state = TextInputState::new();
        input_state.focused = true;
        self.input.render(text_area, &mut frame.buffer, &mut input_state);

        // Set cursor position for terminal
        let (cx, cy) = self.input.cursor_position(text_area);
        frame.set_cursor(cx + 2, cy);
    }
}
```

**Key concepts introduced:**
- Key event handling with pattern matching
- TextInput widget operations
- Cursor position reporting to Frame
- Layout with multiple widgets

---

### Part 4: Tool Status and Indicators

**Goal:** Add agent-style status with spinners and progress.

```rust
//! Part 4: Tool Status
//!
//! Shows spinner animation for running tools and status line.

use ftui::prelude::*;
use ftui_widgets::{LogViewer, TextInput, Spinner, ProgressBar, spinners};

struct AgentHarness {
    log_viewer: LogViewer,
    input: TextInput,
    spinner: Spinner,
    spinner_state: SpinnerState,
    current_tool: Option<String>,
    status: String,
}

// ... implementation showing:
// - Spinner animation via tick
// - Status line with model/tool info
// - Progress bars for long operations
// - Tool start/end state management
```

---

### Part 5: Modal Alt-Screen

**Goal:** Show how to temporarily switch to alt-screen for complex interactions.

```rust
//! Part 5: Modal Alt-Screen
//!
//! Demonstrates the mixed-mode strategy: inline normally,
//! but alt-screen for file picker or other modal UI.

impl AgentHarness {
    fn enter_file_picker(&mut self) -> Cmd<Msg> {
        self.mode = HarnessMode::FilePicker;
        Cmd::enter_alt_screen()
    }

    fn exit_file_picker(&mut self, selected: Option<PathBuf>) -> Cmd<Msg> {
        self.mode = HarnessMode::Normal;
        if let Some(path) = selected {
            self.log_viewer.push(format!("Selected: {}", path.display()));
        }
        Cmd::exit_alt_screen()
    }
}
```

**Key concepts:**
- `Cmd::enter_alt_screen()` and `Cmd::exit_alt_screen()`
- Preserving context across mode switches
- When to use alt-screen (complex modals only)

---

### Part 6: PTY Child Process Capture

**Goal:** Show advanced integration with subprocess output.

```rust
//! Part 6: PTY Subprocess Capture
//!
//! Shows how to capture child process output through ftui
//! while preserving the one-writer rule.

#[cfg(feature = "pty")]
fn run_tool(&mut self, command: &str) -> Cmd<Msg> {
    // Spawn child with PTY
    let (master, slave) = ftui::pty::openpty()?;

    let child = Command::new("sh")
        .args(["-c", command])
        .stdin(slave.clone())
        .stdout(slave.clone())
        .stderr(slave)
        .spawn()?;

    // Read from master, forward to log viewer
    // All output serialized through ftui (one-writer preserved)
    Cmd::stream(master, |line| Msg::ToolOutput(line))
}
```

---

## Key Concepts to Explain

### The One-Writer Rule

> "All bytes that affect terminal state are serialized through a single owner."

**Why this matters:**
- Multiple writers = cursor corruption, escape sequence interleaving
- Inline mode is especially sensitive (scrollback can be corrupted)
- ftui enforces this via the runtime owning terminal I/O

**What happens if violated:**
- Cursor jumps to wrong position
- Colors bleed across regions
- Partial escape sequences render as garbage

**How ftui prevents this:**
- Terminal is owned by runtime during `run()`
- All output goes through `present()`
- PTY capture routes through ftui

### Inline vs Alt-Screen Trade-offs

| Feature | Inline Mode | Alt-Screen Mode |
|---------|-------------|-----------------|
| Scrollback preserved | ✓ | ✗ |
| Full-screen clear OK | ✗ | ✓ |
| Log history after exit | ✓ | ✗ |
| Agent harness ideal | ✓ | ✗ |

**When to use inline:** Agent harnesses, REPLs, log viewers
**When to use alt-screen:** Full dashboards, games, temporary modals

### Cursor Contract

> "After any present(), cursor position is restored per the active policy."

**What this means:**
- In inline mode, cursor returns to input line
- Frame::set_cursor() tells ftui where you want the cursor
- Presenter handles the actual restoration

**How to debug cursor corruption:**
1. Check PTY test output
2. Verify no writes outside present() cycle
3. Look for child process stdout leaks

### Sanitization Policy

> "Untrusted bytes are never executed."

**Why logs are sanitized by default:**
- Tool output may contain ANSI escapes
- Malicious content could inject cursor moves
- Security: prevent terminal escape attacks

**How to opt-in to raw ANSI:**
```rust
log_viewer.push(Text::raw(ansi_string)); // Dangerous!
log_viewer.push(Text::sanitized(user_input)); // Safe (default)
```

---

## Code Examples

Each section should have:
- ✓ Complete, runnable code
- ✓ Explanation of key lines
- ✓ Common mistakes to avoid
- ✓ Performance notes

**Common Mistakes:**

1. **Forgetting to set cursor position:**
   ```rust
   // WRONG: No cursor shown
   fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
       self.input.render(area, &mut frame.buffer, &mut state);
   }

   // RIGHT: Cursor visible at input position
   fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
       self.input.render(area, &mut frame.buffer, &mut state);
       let (x, y) = self.input.cursor_position(area);
       frame.set_cursor(x, y);
   }
   ```

2. **Blocking in update():**
   ```rust
   // WRONG: Freezes UI
   fn update(&mut self, msg: Msg) -> Cmd<Msg> {
       std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_secs(5));
       Cmd::none()
   }

   // RIGHT: Async via commands
   fn update(&mut self, msg: Msg) -> Cmd<Msg> {
       Cmd::perform(async { long_operation().await }, |r| Msg::Done(r))
   }
   ```

3. **Writing to stdout directly:**
   ```rust
   // WRONG: Violates one-writer rule
   println!("Debug: {}", value);

   // RIGHT: Use tracing
   tracing::debug!(value, "Debug");
   ```

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Tutorial covers all 6 parts
- [ ] Each part builds on previous (copy-paste and it compiles)
- [ ] Code examples compile and run
- [ ] Key concepts explained clearly with diagrams
- [ ] Common pitfalls documented with WRONG/RIGHT examples
- [ ] PTY testing mentioned for verification
- [ ] Links to API docs for widgets used
- [ ] Performance notes where relevant

 {"id":"bd-wr2","title":"Implement core widgets (Block, Paragraph, Table)","description":"PHASE 5 - CORE WIDGETS

## Background
Core widgets provide essential UI building blocks. Block is a container with borders/title, Paragraph renders styled text with wrapping, Table displays tabular data.

## Technical Requirements

### Block Widget
- Border styles: None, Single, Double, Rounded, Thick, Custom
- Title positioning: Top/Bottom × Left/Center/Right
- Padding (inner margin)
- Style for border and background
- Child content area calculation

### Paragraph Widget
- Text rendering with word/char wrap modes
- Alignment: Left, Center, Right, Justify
- Scroll offset for long content
- Line height/spacing
- Style inheritance from parent

### Table Widget
- Header row with column titles
- Column constraints (width, alignment)
- Row selection/highlighting
- Cell styling
- Horizontal/vertical scroll
- Fixed header during scroll

## Design Rationale
These three widgets cover the majority of TUI use cases. Block is the universal container, Paragraph handles all text display, Table presents structured data.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Block with all border styles
- [ ] Block title positioning
- [ ] Paragraph word wrap
- [ ] Paragraph alignment modes
- [ ] Table column constraints
- [ ] Table row selection
- [ ] All widgets implement Widget trait
- [ ] Style customization for all components
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests

## Widget Trait
All widgets implement:
pub trait Widget {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer);
}

 {"id":"bd-xh8s","title":"Inline scroll-region + cursor safety for log writes","description":"Problem: Bottom-anchored inline mode can save or restore cursor inside the UI region; log writes may overwrite UI if the cursor is not moved to the log region before writing. Scroll-region boundaries might be incorrect.

Scope:
- Ensure log writes always move the cursor to the log region before writing, regardless of prior save or restore.
- Validate and normalize scroll-region boundaries for both top- and bottom-anchored inline strategies.
- Make cursor save or restore deterministic and never leave the UI region active during log writes.

Testing:
- Unit tests for TerminalWriter log writes in inline top and bottom anchors; assert cursor position and scroll region.
- E2E script streaming logs while UI updates; assert UI stability and no overwrites; include PTY capture and hex dump on failure.
- Additional test verifying scroll-region is reset or cleared on exit.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Log writes never corrupt UI across anchors.
- Scroll-region logic is correct and verified by tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:28:39.481234591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T20:40:51.272912449Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T20:40:51.272894154Z","close_reason":"Implemented cursor safety for log writes in inline mode","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xn2","title":"Create CI/CD pipeline configuration","description":"INFRASTRUCTURE - CI/CD PIPELINE

## Background
Continuous integration ensures code quality, test coverage, and performance budgets are maintained. Must run on every PR and push to main.

## Technical Requirements

### GitHub Actions Workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml)

#### Jobs Overview
| Job | Trigger | Purpose | Blocking |
|-----|---------|---------|----------|
| check | PR, push | Format, clippy, tests | Yes |
| coverage | PR, push | Coverage thresholds | Yes |
| benchmarks | push to main | Performance budgets | Warn |
| e2e | PR, push | End-to-end tests | Yes |
| docs | PR, push | Doc build verification | Yes |

#### Check Job
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test --all-features
- cargo test --no-default-features

#### Coverage Job
- Use cargo-llvm-cov for coverage
- Thresholds:
  - Overall: ≥ 70%
  - ftui-core: ≥ 80%
  - ftui-render: ≥ 75%
- Upload to codecov.io

#### Benchmarks Job
- Run on push to main only
- Performance budgets from plan:
  - Cell comparison: < 1ns
  - Present (80×24, 5% changed): p50 < 1.0ms
  - Diff scan: < 100µs
  - Layout solve (20 items): < 100µs
- Fail on 2x regression

#### E2E Job
- Run scripts/e2e_test.sh
- Upload artifacts on failure
- Test on ubuntu-latest and macos-latest

#### Docs Job
- cargo doc --all-features --no-deps
- Verify no broken links
- Upload docs artifact

### Matrix Testing
- OS: ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest
- Rust: stable, nightly
- Features: default, all, minimal

### Caching
- Cache ~/.cargo/registry
- Cache ~/.cargo/git
- Cache target/ with hash key

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] check job with format/clippy/tests
- [ ] coverage job with thresholds
- [ ] benchmarks job with budgets
- [ ] e2e job with script execution
- [ ] docs job with verification
- [ ] Matrix: 3 OS × 2 toolchains
- [ ] Proper caching configuration
- [ ] Badge in README
